Off Calendar Jul-31-2018 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-30-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Watson and Marc Joyce for and with Parie Lambert (Petitioner); Susan Long (Senior Paralegal) also present.
Steven Handelin for William Santos via Court Call

Mr. Handelin stated that he is listening in to keep tabs on this case to report back to his client, and expressed his appreciation for everyone's efforts. The Court noted several issues re: distribution to post-deceased heirs without 13100 affidavits, petitioner's extraordinary fees to already substantial statutory fees, substantial expense for heir finder, concern of assignees, Public Administrator's former role, and attorney's fees. Mr. Watson stated that this was a unique case with 53 beneficiaries spread across 5 countries/4 continents, that there was a significant amount of work done, the Petitioner entered the house 9 nine months after fact of death, and the Petitioner is not a direct heir. Mr. Joyce stated that time was spent organizing the real property to get it ready for sale, and the declaration submitted categorized only extraordinary fees. Ms. Lambert stated that her husband conducted all online searches. The Court asked if Counsel would like to submit supplemental evidence. Ms. Lambert stated that she initiated the search, worked with her husband, spoke of the 3 years work in progress with respect to case history and family dynamics, and had she known there are 53 beneficiaries she would have left it with the Public Administrator. Mr. Watson stated that he would like to proceed today with what is on the record, he respectfully hears the Court's concerns, his client had faithfully executed the duties, there are no objections from the 53 beneficiaries on the fees, payment of Public Administrator was listed as a liability of Estate, Public Administrator never petitioned and was never appointed, this was a complex case, multiple attorneys have come and gone in his office with a senior paralegal being there for the entire time of this case, and would need to go line by line regarding the Court's concerns with attorney's fees. The Amended First and Final Account, etc. is Taken Under Submission. Counsel to submit revised proposed order to include distributions to pre-deceased heirs' estates, and missing heir's share to be deposited with the county treasurer.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (67 Meadowbrook Dr, San Francisco, Ca 94132)
Off Calendar Aug-31-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Karen Blank for and with Parie Lambert.

The sale was confirmed at $1,250,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $1,250,000.00 is required.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-11-2016 Continued To Nov-22-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marc Joyce (via Court Call) for Parie Lambert.

The Court stated that this is a petition for letters of administration and asked counsel if they are still trying to serve the beneficiaries. Mr. Joyce stated that the genealogist is finishing up, that November 22, 2016 is the continuance date for the general appointment and that they request the special administration be extended to that date. The Court stated that it will continue the Petition for Letters of Administration to November 22, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and that it will sign the order to extend the special letters of administration.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-31-2016 Continued To Oct-11-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marc Joyce for (petitioner) Parie Lambert.

The Court stated that this is a petition for Letters of Administration and that the issue is whether all heirs have been notified. Mr. Joyce replied yes, that there is issue of decedents
first cousins, that there will be an ex parte hearing this morning to deal with this and the vacant property. The Court asked about the 58 first cousins. Mr. Joyce responded that only 12 are living, that 46 are predeceased, that it is issue of the predeceased first cousins that need to be served, that it will cost $20,000.00 to do the genealogical search and that they would like to search the house for a will or trust before spending time and money on the search and notice. The Petition for Letters of Administration was continued to October 11, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)
